title: Sprint 1 front-half memorability and merge safety
summary: Sprint 1 should make the front half more memorable while improving clean-machine safety and lowering merge risk before another wider content wave lands.

execution_order.goal: Prepare Sprint 1 lane-1 merge-safety and seam-split work.
execution_order.why_now: The next broad wave should land on clean machines and a slightly safer coordinator shape.

execution_order.goal: Prepare Sprint 1 lane-2 Coastal Scrub identity support work.
execution_order.why_now: Coastal Scrub still needs a stronger remembered feel than the other live biomes.

execution_order.goal: Prepare Sprint 1 lane-3 coast-side signature pocket work.
execution_order.why_now: The front half should gain one memorable, recoverable place without new traversal language.

execution_order.goal: Prepare Sprint 1 lane-4 route-feel extension work.
execution_order.why_now: Support-readable route feel should spread carefully to one front-half and one forest outing next.

guardrails: Keep handheld text density under the current ceiling.
Do not answer Sprint 1 with a bigger planner or quest shell.
Reuse current traversal language in front-half place work.

main_299_focus.preferred_seam: move the remaining inspect-hint projection layer out of game.ts now that supportBiasActive and nearestInspectableEntityId already live in field-request-controller.ts
recommended_hotspots: game.ts renderGameToText() fieldRequestHint and nearestInspectableEntityId projection
main_299_focus.docs_note: Add one short fresh-machine verification note that names build, test, and agent validation after npm install.
must_preserve: current field-request hint copy and variants

main_300_focus.shape: complete the existing bluff -> swale -> forest-edge memory trio through close-look cards

main_301_focus.target_band: scrub-owned back-dune half just right of the threshold
recommended_shape: one tiny back-dune hold lip only if needed
one compact back-dune hold rest as the remembered shelf
avoid: reopening the Coastal Scrub bluff/swale or shore-pine bands
moving corridor ownership or travel logic

routes.state: ordinary final wrack-line stage on the tide-line shelf
routes.proof: hand-lens already retargets inspect from a nearer pacific-sand-crab to bull-kelp-wrack; the chip should become support-readable there without claiming an active-clue alternate

routes.state: ordinary Root Hollow shelter setup with a nearer non-route decoy still in inspect range
routes.proof: use one deterministic root-hollow shelf where hand-lens retargets inspect from a nearer decoy to a live shelter clue such as sword-fern while note-tabs stays on the normal nearest inspectable

seam_guidance.required_split: Do not overload supportBiasActive. Expose a broader retarget signal for the chip and keep the narrower active-clue meaning separate if later UI still needs it.
seam_guidance.preferred_chip_meaning: hand-lens is changing what E will inspect right now
seam_guidance.bubble_rule: Keep the stronger LENS CLUE bubble text on the narrower active-clue path unless the implementation finds a clear reason to widen it too.

avoid: touching more than the two named route proofs
